title: mcp degraded-signal test brittle: capability-gating auto-resolves lex on CPU-only CI, broke main since Jun 26
description: test/mcp.test.ts:699 'a degraded hybrid recall reports degraded:true' called recall with no lexOnly. Capability-gated recall (landed 2026-06-26, 705877a) made the mode host-dependent: shouldAutoResolve(undefined, warmServer=false)=true -> resolveExplicitMode/autoRecallMode -> on CPU-only CI resolves 'lex' -> effectiveLexOnly=true -> embed barrier skipped -> degraded:false -> assertion 'expected false to be true' fails. Production is CORRECT (don't load a model on a CPU box, spec section 4); the test was brittle. main CI red every run since Jun 26 (only this 1 test). Fix: test requests hybrid explicitly (lexOnly:false) so shouldAutoResolve returns false regardless of host/warm-daemon -> embed barrier always runs -> degraded:true. Verified under QMD_FORCE_CPU=1 (CI condition) + normal + full suite 970/970. Note: delegate-cli warm-daemon tests are shielded by warmServer=true (gate returns false) so they pass in real CI; they only fail under a blunt QMD_FORCE_CPU=1 override (CLI subprocess inherits it -> explicit lex -> lex never delegates).

title: recall-bench distractorFloorPrecision is blind to rescued (below-floor) hits
description: Found during qp-mgm. distractorFloorPrecision() in scripts/recall-bench.ts counts a distractor query 'clean' when res.hits.every(h => (h.score ?? 0) < DEFAULT_MIN_SCORE). A qp-dnx rescued hit is BELOW floor by design (its raw sub-floor score is surfaced), so it passes that predicate — the metric cannot detect a rescue-induced false promotion on a distractor. Latent today (the 3 distractors share no token with any fact, so the rescue never fires on them; verified by A/B probe), but the instrument is structurally unable to catch the failure mode qp-mgm exists to guard. Fix: treat a rescued hit as a floor violation, e.g. clean iff res.hits.every(h => (h.score ?? 0) < DEFAULT_MIN_SCORE) && !res.hits.some(h => h.rescued); add a distractor fixture that shares a pool-common token with a fact to exercise it. Optional larger item: the committed bench has no rescue ON/OFF isolation column (delta default vs QMEMD_RESCUE_DELTA=0) — qp-mgm needed a throwaway A/B probe to measure the rescue's marginal effect + per-query rescue audit; consider a committed model-loading rescue-A/B instrument on this measure-first-instruments branch. Model-free for the metric fix (npm test safe); the A/B instrument is a npm run script.

title: Bring recall-bench.ts wiring under test: extract pure helpers + harden the model-pin guard (adversarial-review G1/G2/G4/G5)
description:
Adversarial review (2026-06-29) root finding: scripts/recall-bench.ts (the bench wiring layer) has ZERO automated tests — every bench-side Done-when rests on manual inspection; npm test never touches it. Highest-leverage remediation: extract pc()/payloadStats()/the provenance-model-pin decision into PURE model-free helpers in test/golden/metrics.ts and unit-test them (closes G1/G2/G4/G5 at once + brings bench logic under npm test).

CORE:
- G1: MF-5 model-pin --check guard is model-free yet untested AND currently dormant — committed recall-baseline.json has no provenance, so only the warn-branch ever runs; the mismatch->exit(1) branch (recall-bench.ts:159-166) has never executed. Extract checkProvenance(baseline,currentEmbed,currentQmd)->{action: ok|warn|fail} pure + unit-test both branches.
- G2: pc()'s Math.round(p*n) reconstructs a WRONG binomial success count for EVEN --runs (medianAggregate averages the two middle runs => half-integer count; e.g. (13/18+14/18)/2=0.75 -> round(13.5)=14 -> CI for 14/18 not 13.5/18). Default runs=5 (odd) hides it; --runs=4/6 is user-reachable + untested. Carry the integer success count alongside the proportion instead of reconstructing, or restrict CI to odd runs.
- G4 (notable): the 'embed/rerank model-pin' compares provenance.embedModel ONLY. qmdVersion (which keys the Qwen3 reranker shipped inside @tobilu/qmd) is captured into provenance (recall-bench.ts:171) but NEVER compared in --check, so a qmd version bump that swaps the reranker passes --check silently — defeating the guard's stated purpose (methodology 3.6). Add base.provenance.qmdVersion !== qmdVersion() comparison (warn or fail).
- G5: gitSha()/qmdVersion() 'unknown' fallback is written into a baseline silently on --update-baseline; --check compares neither field so the non-reproducible degradation is invisible. Warn on --update-baseline if any provenance field resolved to 'unknown'.

MINORS (fold in or defer):
- G3: distractor floor-precision printed bare + gated +/-0.07 over only 3 distractors (Wilson(3,3) lo~0.44); footnote excuses R@k/MRR not floorPrec. (gate partly pre-existing e16 — revisit tolerance for a 3-sample proportion.)
- G6: MDE banner hard-codes 0.30 while n=hq.length is dynamic; grows stale as paraphrase queries are added. Derive MDE from n or assert n stays in the 0.30 band.
- G7: per-query table label truncation (slice/padEnd, recall-bench.ts:80/221) operates on UTF-16 code units not bytes (engine uses truncateToBytes); ASCII-only today.
- payloadStats 'p95' is effectively MAX for n<=20 (ceil(0.95n)-1 = last index for small n) — defensible nearest-rank but the label can mislead; note it.
- --check + --update-baseline passed together => UPDATE silently wins (undocumented precedence); --check against a MISSING baseline throws a raw ENOENT instead of a friendly message.

Source of truth: .superpowers/sdd/progress.md 'ADVERSARIAL REVIEW' section. Cheap confirmed reals (payloadStats even-median, P@K footnote, medianAggregate successAtK coverage) already fixed in 1dfb504.

G4 qmd-version mismatch → fail (exit 1, spec §3.6); G2 non-integer count → CI suppressed not fabricated (§3.7). Adversarial review (18 agents): 5 findings refuted, both judgment calls affirmed, 1 Important must-fix (vacuous tolerance test) + 1 Minor doc off-by-one — both fixed. title: HTTP daemon crashes / OOMs on malformed or oversized local requests
description:
Three CONFIRMED robustness defects on the warm HTTP daemon (mcp/server.ts), each reachable by any local process:

- C14 (server.ts:558): new URL(req.url, base) runs OUTSIDE the handler try, in an async createServer callback, so a malformed request-target throws an unhandled rejection that terminates the daemon. Reproduced with plain curl http://127.0.0.1:8182// (Node delivers req.url=// unnormalized). The parse precedes the isLoopbackHost guard, so the guard gives zero protection. Kills the shared store+model for all clients until restart.
- C15 (server.ts:448): collectBody buffers the whole request body with no size cap / no Content-Length check / chunked accepted, so a large or slow-drip POST OOM-kills the shared daemon.
- C16 (server.ts:621): JSON body of literal null passes parseJsonBody as ok:true; /recall, /remember, /forget then deref it -> TypeError -> caught as 500 with a per-request stack in the daemon log (the qmemd-bzq noise class), not the intended 400.

Fix direction: move new URL inside the try (or validate req.url first); cap collectBody with a max-bytes abort; null-check parsed.value in every REST handler and return 400.

title: Warm daemon serves wrong/empty index — /health rootHash does not prove index identity
description:
Two CONFIRMED silent-recall-loss bugs share one root: the delegation gate (client.ts:79) trusts only sha256(QMD_MEMORY_DIR), which does not prove the daemon opened the same index the CLI writes.

(1) service.ts:24 captureDaemonEnv omits XDG_CACHE_HOME. If the user sets XDG_CACHE_HOME with no explicit QMEMD_DB, the CLI indexes at $XDG_CACHE_HOME/qmemd/index.sqlite but the systemd --user daemon (never sources .bashrc) opens an empty ~/.cache/qmemd/index.sqlite it never populates. /health rootHash still matches, so every hybrid recall delegates and returns hits:[] -> CLI prints No memories found while facts exist on disk.

(2) client.ts:79 / server.ts:584: an index rebuilt while the daemon holds the old inode is never re-adopted (recall path only store.embed()s, never store.update()s). The stores own model-mismatch remedy (delete QMEMD_DB + reindex) induces exactly this: delegated recall permanently misses all facts written since the rebuild until a manual daemon restart.

Fix direction: capture XDG_CACHE_HOME (and QMEMD_DB/QMEMD_EMBED_MODEL) into daemon.env; extend /health to advertise index identity (db path + mtime/generation + embed model) and have client.ts reject on mismatch and fall back to local instead of serving stale/empty results.

title: Degraded lex recall reuses hybrid score-bucketing — most-relevant hit can be dropped
description:
engine.ts:2181: scoreKey and boostOn gate on opts.lexOnly but NOT lexFallback, while runSearch (2057) routes a lexFallback (embed-timeout degraded) recall to searchLex. So a degraded recall buckets raw/normalized BM25 via Math.round(score/RECENCY_TIE_BUCKET) as if it were a calibrated 0..1 rerank score. CONFIRMED: qmd normalizes BM25 to a saturating [0..1) where strong matches compress above 0.9, so the 0.02 bucket collapses genuinely different-relevance hits into ties resolved by recency/overlap; with the default 4x over-fetch a stronger match can be reordered below weaker ones and sliced out of the top-limit. Explicit --lex is fine (it uses raw scores); only the silent degraded fallback misbehaves, on top of an already-degraded recall.

Fix direction: gate scoreKey/boostOn on (opts.lexOnly || lexFallback) so the fallback path uses exact-tie lex semantics.

(1) :308 auditMemory/fixMemory call walkFactFiles with no onUnreadable — dangling symlink / chmod-000 / dir-named-x.md audits clean, exit 0, on the exact corpus doctor is the remedy for. (3) :340 cross-folder slug collision: scanLinkFacts bySlug keyed by slug only — link --fix stamps superseded_by into the shadowed unreachable copy; collision itself never flagged. (4) :484 fixMemory overwrites an existing .bak from a previous run — only pre-fix backup destroyed on non-git stores. (5) :472 idempotency claim false: dangling-clear creates LINK_ONE_SIDED that run 1 already audited past. Fix together — same walkers/surgery seams.

title: doctor fixability is a static table, not derived from fixer capability — --fix wedges forever
description:
doctor.ts:45 FIXABLE is a static per-code set that includes BODY_TEMPLATE_LEAK, but stripBodyLeak returns null when it cannot act (fence-broken file, or a well-fenced body holding a C1-style unstrippable multiline <invoke>). CONFIRMED (reproduced): auditFact flags BODY_TEMPLATE_LEAK [fixable] BEFORE the MISSING_CLOSE early-return, so qmemd doctor directs the operator to --fix; fixContent returns null, fixMemory writes nothing, the re-audit re-reports the same fixable issue, and doctor exits 1 on every run — wedging a pre-commit/preflight gate on tooling that claims self-repair but never delivers.

Fix direction: derive fixability from whether the fixer can actually act on this content (e.g. probe stripBodyLeak / require a well-formed fence pair), or have detector and fixer share one body-extraction so the [fixable] label cannot lie.
close_reason: Fixability derived per file: BODY_TEMPLATE_LEAK probes stripBodyLeak(content)!==null; LINK_ONE_SIDED fixable=target.fenced (locateFences); fixLinks skips unfixable; details honest (repair by hand). FIXABLE set reduced to static-capability codes with rationale comment. +5 tests, 997 green, e2e: doctor/--fix on wedge file exits honest, no .bak, no loop-bait.

title: doctor --limit silently coerces 0/negative/non-numeric while stale/recall reject it
description:
cli:679: doctor uses Math.max(1, parseInt(...)||10) so --limit 0 / abc silently become 10 (negatives -> 1), while stale (cli:475) and recall (cli:313) reject the same input with exit 1 and a clear message (the qmemd-1jt hardening). Same flag, same staleFacts consumer, two contracts. CONFIRMED. A script relying on the documented reject behavior gets different semantics per verb.

Fix direction: reuse the strict shared limit validator across all three verbs.

title: ey3 entirely-leaked-markup rejection missing from server error allowlists — 500/internal instead of 400
description:
engine.ts:1409 throws the deliberate client rejection fact text was entirely leaked tool-call markup — nothing durable to store, with a comment claiming Reject path-free like assertSafeSlug (MCP verbatim / HTTP 400). But that message matches neither the sanitizeToolError prefix allowlist (mcp/server.ts:93) nor the HTTP catch allowlist (server.ts:771), so MCP returns internal error and REST returns 500. CONFIRMED. The caller sees a server fault and retries or files a bug instead of correcting the input the message instructs it to fix.

Root: the client-safe classifier is a hand-maintained string-prefix allowlist duplicated in two places (already grown to 7 prefixes and already missed this message). Fix direction: throw a typed ClientError from the engine and check it once per surface via instanceof, replacing both prefix lists.
close_reason: Introduced ClientError (engine.ts) — a typed client-facing rejection. Converted every currently-allowlisted client throw (unsafe slug, no fact named x3, invalid platform/review_by/ttl, supersedes combos, entirely-leaked body) to throw ClientError, and replaced BOTH hand-synced message-prefix allowlists (mcp/server.ts sanitizeToolError + HTTP catch) with a single 'err instanceof ClientError' check. The entirely-leaked rejection now answers 400 verbatim on MCP + REST instead of 500/internal. +1 http test asserts 400+message; +1 engine test asserts instanceof ClientError. 1017 green.

title: Fix npm CI OIDC Trusted Publishing — release.yml E404s on fresh-version publish
description:
release.yml (publish on GitHub Release published, 'npm publish --access public --provenance', id-token:write) FAILS for every fresh version: v0.5.1 CI run 28169432529 + its --failed re-run both errored 'E404 PUT https://registry.npmjs.org/@ciel2142/qmemd — not found or no permission'. 0.5.1 was absent from the registry, so the 404 is auth-DENIED, not existence-masking. OIDC trusted publishing has NEVER actually published a fresh version — every live version (0.1.0..0.5.0) shipped via MANUAL local 'npm publish' as owner ciel2142. The earlier 'RESOLVED' note was a false positive: a re-run's E403 'cannot publish over 0.5.0' only appeared because 0.5.0 had been published manually first (E403 proves the version exists, not that OIDC auth works).

Root cause (likely): the trusted-publisher binding on npmjs.com is absent or mismatched. npm CLI can neither read nor set it.

Fix (USER, web UI): npmjs.com -> @ciel2142/qmemd -> Settings -> Publishing access -> add GitHub Actions trusted publisher: org/repo Ciel2142/qmemd, workflow file 'release.yml', environment BLANK (release.yml declares no environment:). Confirm the exact workflow filename + that the repo owner matches the OIDC 'sub'. Then cut a throwaway patch release (or re-run a release job for an UNPUBLISHED version) and confirm the publish step goes green WITHOUT a prior manual publish of that version.

Acceptance: a fresh vX.Y.Z publishes to npm purely from CI (with provenance), no manual fallback. Until then, releases use the manual path (npm publish --access public as ciel2142, no provenance). Found during 0.5.1 release.

comments.author: igi21
comments.text: Confirmed at v0.6.0 release (2026-06-30, CI run 28443485582): release.yml now uses NODE_AUTH_TOKEN=secrets.NPM_TOKEN (OIDC abandoned), but the publish step FAILS with npm code ENEEDAUTH because NPM_TOKEN is unset/empty (NODE_AUTH_TOKEN prints blank in the run env). Build + clean-room smoke + version guard all pass; only registry auth fails. Same failure on v0.5.0 and v0.5.1 runs. v0.6.0 was shipped by MANUAL local npm publish --access public as user ciel2142 (npm latest 0.5.0 -> 0.6.0). FIX: set the NPM_TOKEN repo secret to a granular/automation publish token, then re-run; until then every release must be published manually.

title: Frontmatter fence-location grammar mismatch corrupts or silently no-ops writes
description:
setFrontmatterKey (engine.ts:310) and doctor stripBodyLeak (doctor.ts:233) locate the frontmatter fence as the first ---prefixed line ANYWHERE, while parseMemory only recognizes frontmatter at byte 0. On fenceless or hand-edited/adopted facts this diverges. All CONFIRMED (reproduced vs dist):

- C5: a fenceless fact whose body contains --- horizontal rules gets review_by/superseded_by spliced between two rules, corrupting the note, while parseMemory still serves it as body so the key is dead.
- C6: markReviewed (engine.ts:2504) never checks content changed; on a fenceless fact it writes byte-identical bytes, git no-ops, yet returns success -> the fact resurfaces in qmemd stale as due-today forever, reviewed never converges.
- C7: the supersede stamp (engine.ts:1661) silently fails to write superseded_by on a fenceless target, so the retired fact stays active in every recall lane and doctor --fix (same function, bails on no-change) can never close the LINK_ONE_SIDED it perpetually reports.
- C4: stripBodyLeak mistakes two markdown --- rules for the fence pair, mutating a file fixContents own contract says to leave untouched, writing a .bak, and reporting the leak fixed while a token before the bogus fence survives.

Fix direction: one shared locateFences() anchored to byte-0 (matching parseMemory), used by setFrontmatterKey + both doctor sites; callers guard on changed-content before claiming success.
close_reason: C6/C7 fixed in cbccf57 (C4/C5 in c435bf3): locateFences caller-guards — markReviewed throws with repair path on fenceless fact; supersede stamp surfaces supersedeWarning + drops unstamped commit path. Guard is locateFences, not byte equality (idempotent restamp stays success). +3 tests, 992 green, tsc clean, e2e vs dist. PR #5.

comments.author: igi21
comments.text: C4+C5 fixed in c435bf3 (branch fix/frontmatter-fence-location): shared byte-0 locateFences() in engine.ts; setFrontmatterKey + doctor stripBodyLeak rebuilt on it. +6 tests, 989 suite green, tsc clean. REMAINING: C6 (markReviewed reports success on a no-op setFrontmatterKey write) and C7 (supersede stamp silent no-op). Fix: caller-guard on changed content + surface a warning.

title: Temporal + knowledge-update eval track (supersession-retirement + staleness partitions)
description:
e15 from the mem0-borrow NOW slate. Add two MODEL-FREE behavioral eval partitions over a real seeded store, reusing test/golden/seed.ts + metrics.ts. Asserts deterministic MECHANICS, not relevance (judges: <0.05 relevance delta = noise on the 34-fact set). Must stay in 'npm test' (lexOnly, no model load — MF invariant).

Partition A — supersession-retirement (bri): seed A, remember(B,{supersedes:A}); assert A absent from recallQuery hits + moreMatches/belowFloor counters (gate engine.ts:1946, counter engine.ts:2001), absent from recallSession even when pinned (engine.ts:553), absent from staleFacts due+unreviewed (engine.ts:2175); A body byte-preserved + 'updated' unchanged (engine.ts:1548-1573); one commit, both pathspecs; NEGATIVE invariant: a conflicts_with-stamped fact stays fully recallable (only supersededBy hides); fail-open: unreadable retired file is SHOWN (engine.ts:1926-1928).

Partition B — staleness (qmemd-9su): drive staleFacts(root,{today,limit}) with injected today (engine.ts:2163); due fact STILL recalled in same fixture (recall ignores review_by, gate has no review_by branch); review_by:never exempt (engine.ts:2176); TTL project90/reference180/durable (engine.ts:137-139) + QMEMD_TTL_<TYPE> override; boundary cases (==today due; future neither; undatable due dueDate=today engine.ts:2199; malformed fails-open engine.ts:2188-2190); reviewed leaves updated honest (engine.ts:2245); staleFacts NEVER mutates corpus (assert bytes unchanged).

Layout: standalone test/golden/retirement.test.ts + staleness.test.ts reusing seed helpers (decided). Keep seed.test.ts count assertions green (relevance golden set untouched). Additive test code only — zero src/ runtime change.

GATE-FREE per roadmap line 110 (test infra). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-27-now-tranche-eval-spine-and-guardrail.md §1.
close_reason: e15 landed: createTmpMemoryStore helper (d598325) + retirement.test.ts (4 partitions, 7899233) + staleness.test.ts (4 cases, 0a1cbbd). All model-free, in npm test (full suite 890 green, no model load). Reviewer Approved each; 3 strengthening fixes applied.

title: Tier-2 dedup blocks a legitimate write as duplicate of a nonexistent (ghost) fact
description: VERIFIED (3-lens adversarial, reproduced in tmp store). Index can hold a doc whose file is gone: (a) recall --session git pull deletes a fact forgotten on another machine — nothing reindexes on pull; (b) forget() reindex failure is caught-and-logged (engine.ts:2648) leaving the doc active=1. Next remember of similar text: searchLex hit on the ghost, getFact returns null at src/engine.ts:1579, and the fallback ternary at 1582 returns disposition:duplicate with duplicateOf pointing at a slug whose file does not exist — write withheld forever. Fix: treat a null getFact on a Tier-2 hit as a ghost — drop the candidate (optionally queue a reindex) instead of blocking.

title: gitCommit ignores git add exit status — staging failure reported as benign synced:true
description: UNVERIFIED (finder code-read). src/git.ts:117: run(["add","-A","--",...paths]) status discarded. Under index.lock contention (CLI + daemon writing concurrently — supported topology) add exits 128 or times out (null); the follow-up diff --cached --quiet then sees nothing staged and gitCommit returns ok:true, committed:false — indistinguishable from nothing-to-commit. Fact stays uncommitted with a success-shaped result. Fix: propagate add failure into the result (ok:false or reason:add-failed) so callers can surface it; consider retry on index.lock.

title: HTTP daemon has no authentication — local corpus exfil + port-squat recall poisoning
description:
mcp/server.ts:566: the only request gates are isLoopbackHost, isAllowedOrigin (which passes ABSENT Origin), and a POST-only content-type check — no token/secret anywhere on the REST+MCP HTTP surface. CONFIRMED. Threat model in the code comments addresses only browser vectors (DNS rebinding/CSRF); co-resident OS users are unaddressed. Consequences on a multi-user host:

- Corpus exfil: any local account runs curl -H "Host: 127.0.0.1" http://127.0.0.1:8182/list then /get?slug=... and dumps the whole corpus — which the code own guard comment says holds live SSH/DB/Redis/Gitea creds + PATs. /list and /get are GETs so even the content-type gate does not apply.
- Recall poisoning: /health returns rootHash = sha256(memory root path) with no salt, guessable from the username; a process squatting :8182 before the real daemon serves a forged /health the client accepts on hash-equality, then returns fabricated hits into every delegated recall (client.ts:108 already acknowledges this).

Note: severity is medium under the single-user-workstation localhost trust stance the code adopts, but this is qmemd-public and may run on shared hosts, where it is high; the exfil target is exactly the credentials the code names.

Fix direction: add a per-daemon shared secret (random token written to a 0600 file at start, sent as a header by the client) or a peer-credential (SO_PEERCRED / unix-socket) check; salt rootHash or bind to a unix domain socket with filesystem permissions.

title: Hybrid recall silently capped at qmd 40-row rerank pool — completeness footer lies
description: VERIFIED (3-lens adversarial, traced through @tobilu/qmd 2.5.3). src/engine.ts:2119 store.search never passes candidateLimit; qmd hybridQuery slices the RRF fusion pool to RERANK_CANDIDATE_LIMIT=40 before rerank (plus per-source top-20 caps), so runSearch(n) returns at most ~40 rows for any n. With corpus >40 facts + the default project gate, the qmemd-amm refetch at engine.ts:2211-2213 re-fetches the same 40 rows and sets saturated=false, so truncated results are reported complete (no completeness footer). Fix: pass candidateLimit through (qmd SDK supports it) and make the saturation check honest. Related: qp-index-platform-project-fields-021 (over-fetch design).

title: Decide hybrid-by-default vs lex-first recall policy (measure warm cost/benefit)
description:
Measured (golden set, 2026-06-24): lex P@1=0.867 @ ~0ms; hybrid P@1=0.933 @ ~580ms warm (cold 1660ms = model-load artifact). The +0.067 P@1 uplift is arithmetically all from the 3 multi-relevant queries (the 12 single-relevant anchors already get lex P@1=1.0). The golden set is lex-trivial so it understates hybrid's real (semantic) value.

Question: is hybrid-by-default worth +580ms/query on small per-project corpora (lex is ~0ms)?

Tasks:
(a) measure warm lex-vs-hybrid on a REAL corpus (needs the paraphrase/distractor set from R3);
(b) evaluate a lex-first cascade — run lex, escalate to hybrid only when lex returns < N hits or low BM25 confidence;
(c) decide: cascade, keep hybrid default (documented), or corpus-size-aware default.

Acceptance: a measured recommendation + the chosen default wired into recallQuery (engine.ts:1770+). The warm daemon (qmemd-vuk) already hides cold-load, so this is purely the 580ms-vs-0ms steady-state tradeoff.

title: Index platform/project fields to drop recall over-fetch + corpus-wide refetch
description:
platform and project are not index fields, so recallQuery over-fetches fetchLimit = limit * MEMORY_TYPES.length (=4) whenever a type/platform/project gate is active (engine.ts:1781), parses each candidate file to gate (engine.ts:1899-1910), and on a gated underflow refetches the WHOLE corpus and reranks it (engine.ts:1959-1968, qmemd-amm). Indexing platform/project removes the refetch and the O(N) per-file reads.

Note: latency win is modest — qmd caps rerank candidates at topK=40, so cutting fetchLimit helps only past that. The real payoff is correctness/simplicity and scaling as the corpus grows (tens -> hundreds of facts).

Acceptance: gate predicates served from the index; the corpus-wide refetch path removed; recall results unchanged on the golden set.

title: Infra hardening minors: systemd quoting, GPU probe cached-false, rootHash normalization, ssh passphrase stall
description: UNVERIFIED (finder code-read). (1) src/service.ts:94 ExecStart embeds bin/entry unquoted — space or % in install path breaks the unit (systemd word-splitting / specifier expansion); quote and escape, same for EnvironmentFile. (2) src/capability.ts:123 transient GPU probe failure (postinstall rebuild in flight, ENOMEM) cached as permanent gpu:false keyed only by nlcVersion — host stuck on lex until version changes; cache only definitive probes or add TTL/retry. (3) src/client.ts:79 rootHash compares un-normalized path strings — trailing slash / symlinked prefix (/var/home) silently disables delegation forever; normalize (realpath) before hashing on both sides (relates qp-daemon-index-identity-health-0lq). (4) src/git.ts:25 nonInteractiveGitEnv does not cover ssh passphrase prompts — key-with-passphrase + no agent re-creates the per-write 5s stall (qmemd-b3z class); add BatchMode=yes via GIT_SSH_COMMAND.

title: Lazy-import @tobilu/qmd so filesystem-only verbs skip the SDK graph (beacon hot path 58ms->27ms)
description:
engine.ts:6 (and store.ts:3) import @tobilu/qmd eagerly, pulling better-sqlite3 + sqlite-vec native bindings at module load. Every filesystem-only verb — hook beacon (runs on EVERY Bash PreToolUse), recall --session (SessionStart), show/list/tags/stale/doctor/dedup — pays this even though it never opens the store. MEASURED (15 runs, WSL2, warm cache): hook beacon 58ms vs 27ms with the two qmd imports stubbed = ~31ms/call pure waste (~53-66% of node-attributable startup). Highest-frequency path in the product. CONFIRMED; lazy-import precedent already exists in the CLI (await import(../mcp/server.js), capability.ts node-llama-cpp).

Fix direction: keep the type-only import static (free) and dynamic-import the runtime bindings (Maintenance in forget/applyMerge, createStore in openMemoryStore) at first store use; surface qmd resolution errors cleanly on the store-verb path.

title: qmemd: leaked tool-call template markup (</fact>, <parameter name=...>) can enter fact bodies; doctor doesn't detect it
description:
SYMPTOM
Six stored memory facts had leaked tool-call/template markup at the END of their body:
the last content line ended with a stray `</fact>` and the next line was a bare
`<parameter name="type">project`. Discovered during the 2026-07-02 corpus review sweep.

AFFECTED (all now fixed — body stripped, `updated` bumped, in qmd-memory history):
- reference/ofacservice-eu-uk-ofac-list-dates-matched-persons
- reference/bpipelimit-phase-4-hardening-2026-06-22-added-6-new-parity
- reference/cryptoproservice-keystoreservice-getprivatekey-alias-is-the
- reference/psm-adapter-migration-to-configurations-routes-psm-camel
- reference/inavcalculator-spring-boot-2-7-java-version-17-lombok
- reference/qmd-cli-indexing-embedding-gotchas-verified-2026-06-22-on

Signal: ALL six are type=reference. Possibly a specific write path / agent-emission
format leaked its own tool-call wrapper into the `fact` argument (the value captured a
trailing `</fact>` closing tag + the following `type` parameter line). Root mechanism is
likely model-emission/tool-arg-parse at write time, not a storage bug — but qmemd stored
it verbatim (correct per the verbatim/NON-PORT contract) and never flagged it.

TWO GAPS TO CLOSE

1. DETECTION (doctor) — `qmemd doctor` did NOT catch this. doctor audits frontmatter
   integrity (fence/type/name/null-byte/platform/link/review_by divergence) but not
   BODY corruption. Add a body-integrity check that flags leaked template/tool-call
   fragments in the body: `</fact>`, `<fact>`, `^<parameter name=`, `<invoke`, `</invoke>`,
   `<function_calls>`, `</function_calls>`. `--fix` should mechanically strip them
   (writes .bak, same as other doctor fixes). This is the mechanical-repair subset.

2. PREVENTION (remember) — the write path should defend: on `remember`/`--replace`,
   detect these fragments in the incoming fact text and either strip-with-warning or
   reject (surface, don't silently store). Keep it model-free (I1/I5). Do NOT over-reach
   into general HTML — target only the known tool-call/template tokens.

ACCEPTANCE
- doctor flags all 6 patterns above in a body; `doctor --fix` strips them cleanly.
- remember refuses-or-sanitizes a fact whose text contains them (with a clear message).
- a regression test seeds a corrupted body and asserts detect + fix + write-guard.

WORKAROUND (done this session): corpus-wide `grep -rl '</fact>\|<parameter name='` then
strip the trailing tag + delete the leaked line + bump `updated`. Corpus now 0 corrupted.
close_reason: done

title: MCP CLI daemon-lifecycle hazards: pidfile kill, silent --daemon, --port swallow, source install-service
description:
Cluster of CONFIRMED CLI arg/lifecycle gaps around qmemd mcp:

- cli:567 mcp stop guards the pidfile only against NaN; a pidfile of 0 makes process.kill(0, SIGTERM) signal the callers whole process group (kills the CLI + parent shell/CI job), and -N signals group N. Also cli:627: pid 0 makes kill(0,0) always succeed -> Already running (PID 0) forever.
- cli:622 qmemd mcp --daemon WITHOUT --http silently ignores --daemon (nested under if values.http) and falls into the blocking stdio server: no port, no pidfile, no error — a silent hang.
- cli:623 and cli:578 --port uses Number(x)||...||8182, so --port 0 (documented ephemeral mode) and non-numeric typos silently collapse to 8182; install-service bakes the wrong port into the systemd/launchd unit with no error (the qmemd-1jt falsy-|| class already fixed for --limit).
- cli:584 install-service passes entry=fileURLToPath(import.meta.url) with no .ts handling (unlike the --daemon spawn which injects tsx), so npm run qmemd -- mcp install-service writes ExecStart=node .../src/cli/qmemd.ts, which Restart=always crash-loops every 2s.

Fix direction: validate pid>0 before kill; error on --daemon without --http; reject non-integer/zero --port (reuse the --limit validator); guard the .ts entry in install-service like the --daemon path does.

title: Lazy MCP store caches a rejected promise forever — one transient failure bricks the session
description: UNVERIFIED (finder code-read). src/mcp/server.ts:441: storeP ??= openMemoryStore() memoizes the promise and never clears it on rejection. First store-backed tool call failing transiently (better-sqlite3 binding mid-build on npx clean-room launch — the exact scenario connect-first targets; $QMEMD_DB locked by concurrent CLI; cache dir not creatable) leaves every subsequent store-backed call rejecting with the stale error until process restart. Fix: clear storeP on rejection so the next call retries.

title: Honest stats layer in recall-bench: Wilson CI + per-query table + paired McNemar + MDE banner + prohibited-claims
description:
Methodology items 1+6 (measure-first tranche; builds on CLOSED e16/38c + e15/qp-gep). Make the recall scorecard refuse to over-claim.

Pure, model-free (unit-tested in metrics.test.ts):
- wilson(successes,n,z=1.96) -> {lo,hi}: Wilson score 95% CI. Methodology 3.1 — a bare proportion is not a permitted output. At P@1=0.778,n=18 the CI is ~+/-0.19, which swallows the lex-vs-hybrid delta.
- mcnemar(pairs) -> {b,c,statistic,pApprox}: continuity-corrected paired test (3.2 — hybrid-vs-lex is PAIRED; two separate CIs are wrong). lex 13/18 vs hybrid 14/18 = net one query, p~1.0.

Bench (scripts/recall-bench.ts, model-loading, bench:recall only):
- Wilson CI beside every proportion (rowOf:57, hybrid line:87).
- Replace bare Delta row (:89) with paired McNemar delta; extend runMode (:30-42) to return perQueryHit1 = scores.map(s=>s.pAt1===1).
- MDE banner at start (n=18 -> MDE ~0.30 @ 80% power).
- Per-query disaggregation table (query | lex@1 | hybrid@1 | rank).
- Prohibited-claims comment block (the 7 hard-NOs, methodology 3.7).

MF invariant preserved: metrics.ts stays pure; bench-only model load. Highest value in the tranche.

Spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-28-measure-first-instruments.md sec 1+6. Plan docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-28-measure-first-instruments.md PART A.

Verified in code 2026-07-04.

title: Validate tag-overlap rescue precision/recall on real corpus (hybrid bench)
description: qp-dnx shipped model-free (fake-store + pure units). Validate on the REAL corpus via npm run bench:recall (model-loading): does the rescue/tie-break improve R@k/MRR without false promotions? Use golden hybrid + distractor queries. If precision drops, upgrade the static stoplist to pool-DF over the ≤40 in-hand candidates (NOT corpus-DF — keep tests deterministic), per consilium chair adjudication. Also: stemming gap (reranker≠slug rerank exact-token miss) — revisit only if eval shows it matters.
close_reason: VALIDATED on real corpus (golden hybrid set, embeddinggemma-300M + Qwen3-Reranker-0.6B) + FIXED. Method: npm run bench:recall (canonical) + a throwaway ON/OFF A/B probe (flip QMEMD_RESCUE_DELTA in-process, 5 rounds × {k=5, limit=10}, per-query rescue audit) + a pool-DF measurement dump. Findings: qp-dnx rescue gave 0 measurable R@k/MRR lift (Δ ON−OFF = 0.000; the false-empty failure mode it targets isn't in this golden set) and exactly 1 DETERMINISTIC false promotion — 'k3s systemd labhost' rescued grafana-dashboards-live-on-the-k3s-sandbox (raw 0.528, 5/5 rounds) on the lone shared token 'k3s'. The committed distractorFloorPrecision metric stayed 1.000 because it is rescue-blind (filed qp-bench-distractor-rescue-blind-duh). Pool-DF measurement showed clean separation: every false rescue-candidate overlapped ONLY pool-common tokens (k3s df2, port df6, model/review df2); every relevant fact carried ≥1 pool-unique (df1) token. Fix per chair steer: pool-DF over the ≤40 in-hand candidates (NOT corpus-DF). A query-overlap token counts toward distinctiveOverlap only if its DF across the gated above+below-floor pool is ≤ RECALL_POOL_DF_MAX (1 = unique-in-pool); static stoplist kept as the universal floor. New exports poolDocFreq + RECALL_POOL_DF_MAX; distinctiveOverlap gains optional poolDf (legacy when omitted); recallQueryWithStatus builds the map once (boost on) and threads it into tie-break + rescue. NOT stoplisting 'k3s' (legit domain token) and NOT overlap≥2 (would kill single-distinctive-token rescues like JAVA_HOME). Post-fix A/B: 0 false promotions, Δ=0.000 both limits, distractors clean, --check green. TDD model-free +9 tests, suite 963 green, build clean. Commit ff163c3. Stemming-gap sub-note: not pursued — eval shows no slug exact-token miss mattered. Stoplist-breadth (review M2): resolved by pool-DF, no static expansion needed.

title: Bootstrap/t-interval CI for MRR and graded R@k in recall-bench (non-binomial dispersion)
description: MF-1/A2 scoped the Wilson CI to true binomial proportions only (P@1, success@k). MRR (mean of reciprocal ranks) and aggregate R@k (golden set mixes 12 single-relevant + 3 graded multi-relevant queries) are means of per-query ratios, NOT binomials — a Wilson CI on them is false precision (the very thing methodology 3.7 bans), so they now print bare with a footnote. Follow-on: give MRR and graded R@k an HONEST dispersion interval via a model-free nonparametric BOOTSTRAP (resample the per-query reciprocal-rank / recall values, percentile CI) or a t-interval. Pure + unit-testable in metrics.ts (like wilson/mcnemar). Then surface in recall-bench rows replacing the bare-value footnote. Keep it model-free (npm test safe). Display-only; does not touch the baseline or --check.
close_reason: Added pure model-free bootstrapMeanCI (seeded mulberry32 → deterministic percentile bootstrap) in metrics.ts + 6 unit tests; wired R@k + MRR bench rows to render a percentile bootstrap 95% CI (means of per-query ratios — honest dispersion per §3.1 without the §3.7 Wilson false-precision). P@K stays bare/report-only (out of scope). Display-only: baseline + --check untouched. Adversarial review (20 agents): both judgment calls (bootstrap-is-honest, fixed-seed) affirmed, PRNG/percentile math + MF + baseline-untouched all verified; 2 Important fixed — false 'deterministic' comment on the median-value/last-run-CI pairing (reworded to honest query-sampling-dispersion framing) + vacuous tests (added STRICT-interior assertions that fail a {min,max} stub). npm test 928 green/MF; build clean; bench typechecks; --check green end-to-end with CIs rendered.

title: Multiline <invoke> leak makes a fact permanently unstorable
description:
engine.ts:950 the <invoke> leak pattern /<invoke\b[^>]*>/ uses a negated class that matches newlines, so it detects a multiline <invoke\n...> span across the whole text, but stripLeakedMarkup (engine.ts:~979) runs line-by-line and can never remove it. The 100-pass strip loop exits with the token present, cleaned text stays non-empty (no early reject), then the post-condition at engine.ts:1646 throws internal: leaked markup survived sanitization before writeFileSync. CONFIRMED, reproduced vs dist: the fact is permanently unstorable (every retry fails identically), and over MCP it surfaces as opaque internal error.

Root: detection table (LEAK_PATTERNS) and the strip chain are two hand-synced copies; any detect-only pattern reproduces this. Fix direction: drive strip from the same table (each pattern carrying a strip mode), or make detection line-scoped to match the stripper, and make the survivor case a client-facing rejection rather than an internal throw.
close_reason: Stripper now removes a newline-spanning <invoke …> tag on the whole text (engine.ts stripLeakedMarkup: out.replace(/<invoke\b[^>]*>/g, m => m.includes('\n')?'':m)), so detection (whole-text [^>]*) and stripping agree again — the fact strips + writes instead of surviving 100 passes to the post-condition. Survivor post-condition changed from an internal 500 throw to a client-facing ClientError (400) 'strip the framing tokens and retry'. Doctor now derives this case fixable via the existing qp-wex capability probe (stripBodyLeak); updated its test + stale comment. e2e: CLI remember of a multiline-invoke fact now stores cleanly (verified vs dist). +4 tests, 1017 green.

title: Fact writes are non-atomic — crash mid --replace destroys the only copy
description: VERIFIED (3-lens adversarial; sandbox kill between truncate and write left a 0-byte file). src/engine.ts:1685 (also 1706 supersede back-link rewrite, 2550, 2729) uses bare writeFileSync (O_TRUNC then write). On --replace and the back-link rewrite the pre-existing content is truncated first, so SIGKILL/OOM/power loss destroys the previous fact body. Git is optional (repo-gated best-effort), so on git-less stores the fact is unrecoverable. Fix: write temp file in same dir + fsync + rename for every fact write.

title: Configure npm Trusted Publisher for @ciel2142/qmemd before first release-triggered publish
description: release.yml publishes via OIDC Trusted Publishing (no NPM_TOKEN). Until the trusted publisher is registered on npmjs.com, the publish job fails auth. One-time manual step (user, web UI): npmjs.com -> @ciel2142/qmemd -> Settings -> Trusted Publishing -> add GitHub Actions publisher: org/repo Ciel2142/qmemd, workflow file 'release.yml' (environment optional). After that, publishing = create a GitHub Release with tag vX.Y.Z (package.json must already be bumped to match; the workflow guards it). Requires npm CLI >=11.5 on the runner (release.yml does 'npm install -g npm@latest'). Ref: docs.npmjs.com trusted publishers / GitHub OIDC.
close_reason: Obsolete: release.yml abandoned OIDC trusted publishing (now NODE_AUTH_TOKEN=secrets.NPM_TOKEN, no provenance — see workflow comment). Registering a trusted publisher is moot unless OIDC is revived; live auth problem tracked in qp-fix-npm-ci-oidc-trusted-publishing-nbs.

title: parseMemory key switch is case-sensitive — Type:/Review_by: silently dropped, doctor blesses it
description:
engine.ts:~400: the frontmatter key regex is /^([a-z_]+):\s*(.*)$/i (case-insensitive) but the captured key is never lowercased and the switch cases are lowercase literals with no default, so a capitalized key (Type:, Review_by:) matches the line regex yet falls through the switch silently. fm.type stays the default reference (wrong staleness window, wrong glyph, wrong index metadata); fm.reviewBy is lost (fact drops to never-reviewed lane, schedule gone). doctor inspect() (doctor.ts:119) lowercases keys, so it validates a value parseMemory never saw and reports the file clean — blessing the exact divergence it exists to catch. CONFIRMED. Asymmetry: miscased Name:/Supersedes: ARE caught (they compare against the parseMemory decode), so type/review_by are uniquely blind.

Fix direction: lowercase the captured key before the switch in parseMemory (the /i flag signals case-insensitive keys were intended); or drop the toLowerCase in inspect and have doctor flag miscased keys as a divergence.
close_reason: parseMemory now lowercases the captured frontmatter key before the switch (engine.ts ~431), honoring the regex's existing /i flag. A miscased key (Type:, Review_by:) is decoded instead of matching the line yet falling through the lowercase-literal switch — so fm.type/fm.reviewBy are no longer silently lost (wrong staleness window, wrong glyph, dropped schedule). Closes the divergence where doctor's already-lowercasing inspect() blessed a file parseMemory had mis-decoded. +1 test, 1022 green.

title: Pre-due warm daemon leaks foreign-project facts unfenced across an npm upgrade
description:
client.ts:92 always sends project/crossProject when set, but the only capability handshake is rootHash equality, which marks the OLDER platform param, not the later project gate (qmemd-due). No version field in /health distinguishes a due-aware daemon. CONFIRMED: a pre-due dist kept warm by systemd Restart=always after an npm upgrade ignores body.project and returns whole-corpus hits. client.ts maps each hit project as h.project ?? global; a pre-due daemon omits the field, so every foreign hit is relabeled global, which makes printRecallResult isForeign false -> the foreign facts render in the main list with no foreign fence and no divider. A cross-project leak (e.g. another repo credentials) with no visual tell, in the window an old daemon outlives an upgrade.

Fix direction: add a capability/version field to /health and have the client fall back to local (or refuse to render unfenced) when the daemon predates the due scoping contract. Related: qp-daemon-index-identity-health-0lq (same /health-capability gap).

title: Recalibrate DEFAULT_MIN_SCORE against a real calibration curve (not anecdote)
description:
DEFAULT_MIN_SCORE=0.575 (engine.ts:1621) was set by anecdote — lowered from 0.6 to keep one real fact observed at 0.581. Measured on the synthetic golden set: reranker is bimodal (noise ~0.500 tail 0.578, relevant ~0.731); clean window (0.578, 0.690], midpoint ~0.63; 0.575 admits 2/290 noise while any floor in [0.600,0.675] keeps the same 15/18 relevant with 0 noise. BUT 0.63 would drop the documented 0.581 fact, and rerankScore is non-deterministic — so do NOT bump the number blind.

Tasks: derive the floor from R3's real-corpus curve; pick a value maximizing margin from both clusters given run-to-run jitter; document the basis in the code comment (replace the n=1 anecdote).

Acceptance: floor value backed by a measured curve, not a single observation. Blocked by the R3 harness.

comments.author: igi21
comments.text: Real-corpus corroboration (2026-06-30; qmemd memory slug qmemd-recall-trigger-over-firing-investigation-2026-06-30): replayed 155 floor-suppressed PRODUCTION queries (from 4001 transcripts) at minScore:0 cross_project:true + relevance-labeled top-3. CONFIRMS this bead synthetic curve on real data: bimodal — distractor/noise ~0.50-0.55, relevant facts cluster 0.71-0.731. False-suppression at 0.575 = 0.65% (1/155); the only relevant fact below floor scored 0.51 on a vague CRL/perf query. IMPLICATION: 0.575 is NOT too high (no over-reject) AND raising it into the [0.600,0.675] candidate window WOULD drop real relevant-tail facts observed passing at 0.578/0.598/0.610/0.613/0.647/0.653 — the zero-noise gain costs real recall. RECOMMENDATION: keep DEFAULT_MIN_SCORE=0.575; remaining work is replacing the n=1 anecdote comment at engine.ts:1621 to cite BOTH curves (synthetic golden-set + this real-corpus 155-query labeling). Caveats: replayed vs today corpus (drift, 0.65% is a lower bound); cross_project:true inflated the noise tail vs production project-scoped recall; borderline not counted as suppression.

title: Investigate true-corpus-absence as the dominant recall-miss (best_relevant_score=-1)
description: Floor labeling (2026-06-30) found the dominant cause of empty/unhelpful recalls is NOT trigger over-firing (~6.6%) nor an over-aggressive floor (0.65% false-suppression) but best_relevant_score=-1 = the relevant fact is simply ABSENT from the corpus or not retrieved into top-k. The felt wasted recall is genuine coverage gaps, not eagerness/calibration. Investigate: are durable facts captured when they should be (write/capture under-firing)? Does retrieval (lex+vec) miss facts that DO exist? Quantify -1 queries: fact existed-but-not-retrieved vs never-stored. This is the real lever, distinct from the refuted over-firing premise. Ref qmemd memory qmemd-recall-trigger-over-firing-investigation-2026-06-30.

title: Silent hybrid-to-lex downgrade invisible in MCP output and CLI --json
description: UNVERIFIED (finder code-read). (1) src/mcp/server.ts:302 — stdio MCP recall on CPU-only host auto-resolves to lex; tool description promises hybrid default, but neither text nor structuredContent carries mode/degraded marker. (2) src/cli/qmemd.ts:372 — gate `if (lexOnly && !values.json)` suppresses the downgrade note exactly when --json is set, contradicting the adjacent comment (stderr only, so --json output is untouched). Agent/script consumers cannot distinguish full hybrid recall from BM25-only. Fix: add mode field to structuredContent + JSON output; emit the stderr note regardless of --json.

title: Hybrid recall has no degraded fallback when corpus fully embedded — broken model hangs/crashes recall
description:
engine.ts:2073: the QMEMD_EMBED_TIMEOUT_MS -> lex degradation only wraps the pre-search vector-backfill barrier (runs when needsEmbedding>0). On a fully-embedded corpus the barrier is skipped, lexFallback can never be set, and store.search is called bare — it loads the generate + rerank models and embeds the query with NO timeout at any layer and no outer catch. CONFIRMED: a broken model runtime (deleted GGUF, driver update, broken native binding) throws out of recall (CLI has try/finally only, no lex retry); a stalled cold load hangs recall indefinitely. The engines own comment admits a hybrid search would re-attempt the same model load and hang or throw yet the guard lives only inside the vectorsPending>0 branch.

Fix direction: wrap the hybrid store.search in the same raceTimeout + lex-fallback envelope regardless of vectorsPending, so a model failure degrades to lex (flagged degraded) instead of hanging/crashing.

comments.author: igi21
comments.text: Adversarially confirmed 2026-07-13 (4x REAL votes, code trace through @tobilu/qmd 2.5.3): raceTimeout+lexFallback envelope exists only inside the vectorsPending>0 barrier branch (engine.ts:2048-2079); steady-state hybrid recall awaits store.search bare at engine.ts:2119, and qmd hybridQuery awaits expandQuery/embedBatch/rerank with no timeout or fallback (llm.js:1056/1148). Model stall hangs recall indefinitely.

title: remember push never reconciles a diverged remote (silent non-fast-forward backlog)
description: Cause diagnosed live 2026-06-30: the qmd-memory repo (gitea service/qmd-memory) had diverged ahead-30/behind-4 because another machine (Mac) pushed 4 remember-commits and THIS workstation's qmemd remember path never pulls. git.ts runs gitPullFfOnly ONLY on session-snapshot paths (recall --session / session:true), NOT on remember. So remember commits locally + best-effort-pushes, but once origin is ahead the push rejects non-fast-forward (exit 1, logged as 'git push failed (push-failed (status 1))') and the local backlog grows unbounded + easy-to-miss. Net effect: facts written on one machine silently stop propagating cross-machine — the exact durability guarantee qmemd sells. Manual reconcile that fixed it: cd $QMD_MEMORY_DIR; git stash; git pull --rebase origin main; git push; git stash pop; qmemd reindex (the rebase was conflict-free — distinct facts from distinct machines rarely collide). Proposed fix (best-effort envelope, never-throw, 5s-timeout preserved): on a non-fast-forward push rejection in the remember path, attempt one git pull --rebase then re-push; if the rebase hits a conflict, abort it and leave local as-is (push stays failed = today's behavior, no regression). Clean-divergence (the common case) then auto-heals. Alternatives: an explicit 'qmemd sync' verb (pull --rebase + push) for cron, or a periodic ff-pull. Also worth: surface the ahead/behind backlog count in 'qmemd status' / doctor so a stalled push is visible, not buried in stderr. Note pulled-in facts arrive via git (not remember) so they are NOT lex-indexed until 'qmemd reindex' — a sync verb should reindex after pull.
notes: Fixed in git.ts gitPush (qp-...): on a nonzero (rejected) push, attempt one pull --rebase + re-push (new reconcileAndRepush). Clean divergence auto-heals; rebase conflict or auth/offline pull-fail => rebase --abort + report ORIGINAL push-failed (no regression). Guarded: a null push status (git dead/timeout) does NOT reconcile (avoids a 2nd 5s timeout). Reason 'reconciled' on success; 'push-failed (status N, after reconcile)' when re-push fails. Fix is confined to git.ts — all 4 write paths (remember/reviewed/forget/merge) heal via the shared gitPush. Reindex-after-reconcile is FREE: every write path already runs reindexMemory (=store.update full re-scan) AFTER gitPush, so foreign facts the rebase lands on disk are adopted with no engine change. Tests: 5 unit (scripted push seq) + 2 real-git integration (diverged remote heals + keeps both commits + foreign file on disk; add/add conflict aborts clean, local commit intact). 970/970 pass, build clean.
close_reason: done

title: Write path has no serialization: TOCTOU slug collision loses a fact silently
description: VERIFIED (3-lens adversarial, reproduced deterministically). src/engine.ts:1541-1685: no lock or O_EXCL between Tier-1 getFact (1541) and writeFileSync (1685); real async yield at await store.searchLex (1562) plus O(n) scanFacts. Two concurrent writers (parallel agent sessions each with own MCP server, or CLI + daemon — SQLITE_BUSY comment at 1718 shows concurrent writers are contemplated) remembering distinct facts that slugify to the same slug both pass Tier-1, both report wrote:true, last write silently wins. UNVERIFIED sibling surface: src/mcp/server.ts:725 — warm daemon dispatches concurrent REST /remember over one shared store, same check-then-write race intra-process. Fix: per-slug lock or O_EXCL create + retry-with-uniquified-slug; serialize remember on the daemon.

title: REST /recall limit coercion: {limit:null} silently caps recall to 1 hit
description:
mcp/server.ts:643: limit is coerced with Number(), so {"limit":null} (the common unset idiom) becomes 0, passes Number.isFinite, and Math.max(1,Math.floor(0)) = 1. CONFIRMED (reproduced): the caller gets exactly one hit with moreMatches>0 making it look like legitimate top-N truncation, so an agent proceeds on one fact and misses the other ~9 relevant memories. null/empty-string/false/[] all collapse to 1; only undefined reaches the engine default of 10.

Fix direction: treat non-number limit (null/bool/etc.) as unset -> engine default, matching the intent of the adjacent comment; or reject a non-numeric limit with 400 as minScore already does.

title: REST /remember skips MCP zod type-checks — pin/project silently corrupt the fact
description:
mcp/server.ts:711: REST /remember type-checks fact/type/tags/platforms/ttl/reviewBy but passes pin, project, as, replace, supersedes, source VERBATIM, unlike the MCP zod layer. CONFIRMED, both reproduced vs dist: pin:"yes" -> serialized pinned: yes -> parseMemory reads val===true -> false, so the fact is silently NOT pinned though wrote:true; project:123 -> serialized and read back as string "123" (an object becomes [object Object]), scoping the fact to a name no basename(cwd)/global gate ever matches -> permanently invisible to every recall. The memory is stored yet effectively lost, no warning.

Fix direction: apply the same type validation MCP zod enforces to pin (boolean) and project/as/replace/supersedes/source (string) in the REST handler; reject with 400 on mismatch.

title: Retired (superseded) facts still act as dedup candidates — reverted value cannot be re-remembered
description: UNVERIFIED (finder claims repro; adversarial verify pass did not reach it). src/engine.ts:1402: fact A superseded by B stays on disk hidden from recall, but Tier-1 getFact resolves the retired A (same slug) and Tier-2.5 scanFacts has no supersededBy filter (unlike dedup.ts walkFacts:115 which excludes retired). When a value reverts (remember the original text again), the write is blocked as duplicate-of-retired while recall keeps serving the wrong successor. Fix: exclude superseded facts from all dedup tiers, or route a retired-slug hit to un-supersede/replace.

title: Review follow-ups: minor CONFIRMED correctness + UX defects
description:
Lower-severity CONFIRMED findings from the max-effort whole-repo review, batched (each is small and independent):

- CLI client-error UX (cli:787): remember/forget/show have no per-verb catch, so curated path-free engine errors (no fact named X, invalid ttl, unsafe slug, supersedes cannot...) dump a raw stack on the CLI while reviewed/dedup-apply print e.message and MCP/REST return clean 400s. Route them to e.message + exit 1.
- Merge command shell-injection (dedup.ts:359): buildMergeCommands interpolates unionTags (arbitrary strings, MCP-writable) unquoted into a paste-to-run qmemd remember ... skeleton; a tag with a space splits argv, one with $(...)/backticks executes on paste. Shell-quote all interpolated values.
- projectOverview counts superseded facts (engine.ts:2548): listFacts includes retired facts, so the beacon N memories count and qmemd tags over-report what recall (which hides supersededBy) can return. Exclude supersededBy in projectOverview.
- Daemon-delegated hits drop rescued (client.ts:113): toHitDTO serializes rescued:true but tryDaemonRecall omits it, so below-floor hits lose the below-floor marker on the default warm-daemon path and --json diverges from local. Map the field.
- Hook exit propagation (run-qmemd.mjs:44): the exit handler forwards the child non-zero code despite the fail-open contract; only the spawn error event is masked. Also the npx -y fallback runs per Bash call when qmemd is off PATH. Force exit 0 on any child exit.
- Git sync freezes the daemon event loop (git.ts): the GitRun seam is spawnSync by type; every daemon-side commit/push/pull (7-9 sequential subprocesses) blocks the event loop, and reconcileAndRepush chains 3 timeouts (~10-15s worst case) past the documented 5s bound, so concurrent /health probes miss the 250ms budget and recall silently goes cold. Consider an async runner or worker offload.
- Dead flag (cli:210): the dedup --report boolean parses and no-ops; delete it and the stale comment.

title: Session snapshot serves stale data silently when ff-only pull FAILS (diverged branch)
description: UNVERIFIED (finder code-read). src/git.ts:229: sessionSyncWarning returns null for every reason except git-unavailable. Diverged local branch (hand commit + remote push from another machine) makes git pull --ff-only fail at every session start forever; gitPullFfOnly returns ok:false reason pull-failed, but recall --session / MCP session / REST session all render the snapshot with no warning. Two-machine sync — the feature point — silently stops. Fix: surface pull-failed (and timeout) reasons in the snapshot warning line.

title: SessionStart hooks emit raw stdout / no suppressOutput → visible 'hook success:' banner
description:
Plugin SessionStart hooks inject context but trigger the visible 'hook success:' banner that crowds the user's first prompt (qmemd memory: claude-code-sessionstart-hook-success-banners-visible). Two emitters:
1. hooks/inject-rule.mjs buildSessionContext() emits {hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:SessionStart,additionalContext}} — MISSING suppressOutput:true.
2. CLI 'qmemd recall --session' (src/cli/qmemd.ts ~293) does console.log(out) RAW — no JSON envelope at all; run-qmemd.mjs passes it straight through.
Fix: wrap both as {suppressOutput:true,hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:SessionStart,additionalContext:<text>}}. Pre-existing on main (old session-start.sh was also raw); PR #1 made the rule net-better but did not add suppressOutput. recallSession() engine string contract unchanged (only CLI stdout wrapping). TDD: cli.test.ts + plugin.test.ts.
close_reason: Wrapped both SessionStart emitters in {suppressOutput:true, hookSpecificOutput.{hookEventName:SessionStart, additionalContext}}: hooks/inject-rule.mjs buildSessionContext() + CLI 'recall --session' (src/cli/qmemd.ts). recallSession() engine string unchanged. TDD: +1 plugin.test.ts (suppressOutput), +2 cli.test.ts (envelope + empty-snapshot). Full suite 858/0. Verified on built dist: both emit suppressOutput=true SessionStart envelope.

title: Measure session-snapshot hit-rate / injection-precision before tuning projectLimit/budget
description:
recallSession (engine.ts:511) selects facts by recency only (+ manual pins); projectLimit=5 and budget=2000 bytes are arbitrary defaults with no measurement (engine.ts:512-513). With 53 project facts for this repo only the 5 newest surface at session start — automatic relevance plays no role until an explicit recall. Snapshot hit-rate / injection-precision and net-context ROI (memory ON vs OFF) are unbuilt axes.

Tasks: build a snapshot injection-precision metric (did injected facts match what the session actually needed?); only then tune projectLimit/budget or add a relevance signal to the snapshot.

Acceptance: a measured snapshot hit-rate baseline + a data-backed projectLimit/budget recommendation.

title: Session-snapshot truncation trail says `qmd recall` — command does not exist
description: VERIFIED (3-lens adversarial; qmd 2.5.3 prints Unknown command: recall). src/engine.ts:780 emits (N more — `qmd recall` to see) when the byte budget drops facts; sibling footers at 641/645/775 correctly say qmemd. An agent following the hint fails and never pulls the dropped facts, defeating the truncation-trail purpose. One-word fix + pin with a test.

title: success@k in scoreQuery/aggregate + relabel P@K report-only
description:
Methodology item 2 (measure-first). Model-free.

P@K is structurally capped at ~0.2 (15/18 queries single-relevant; baseline pAtK=0.144) — reads as "recall broken" when hit-rate@k is ~0.94. Methodology 2A/3.7(g): never present P@K without success@k; P@K measures authoring not ranking — report-only, never gate.

metrics.ts: add successAtK (= hitsInK>0?1:0, free — hitsInK already computed at :26) to QueryScore(:6)/scoreQuery(:24), AggregateScore(:13)/aggregate(:38)/medianAggregate(:55). Relabel pAtK comment report-only-never-gate. Surface success@k in rowOf(:57)/hybrid line(:87) and golden-recall.test.ts:45. Unit-test in metrics.test.ts.

Baseline gains successAtK on next --update-baseline (deferred model run); --check stays back-compat (reads only pAt1/mrr/distractorFloorPrecision :116-118, no crash on missing field). MF preserved.

Spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-28-measure-first-instruments.md sec 2. Plan docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-28-measure-first-instruments.md PART B.
close_reason: Shipped in measure-first-instruments PR #4 (merge 887d113): wilson/mcnemar/MDE/per-query/prohibited-claims (6tc); successAtK end-to-end + baseline 8afe2b8 (c6w); recall-readonly.test.ts cdb4e02 (anh); byte-payload 3c1f470 + session-economy.test.ts 21c1d41 (6ma). Verified in code 2026-07-04.

title: remember --supersedes silently overwrites an unrelated fact on slug collision
description: VERIFIED (3-lens adversarial, reproduced end-to-end in sandbox). src/engine.ts:1498: supersedes skips ALL dedup tiers including the Tier-1 slug-existence check (line 1533); only self-supersede is guarded (1469). If the derived slug collides with an unrelated fact (realistic via 60-char slugify truncation, lines 185-187/1545-1547), writeFileSync at 1685 replaces the victim body, reports wrote:true with no warning, writes into the victim type folder — and if the victim was retired, the new fact inherits superseded_by and is born hidden from all recall surfaces. Fix: run the slug-existence check on the supersedes path; on collision either uniquify the slug or surface a conflict.
close_reason: remember --supersedes no longer overwrites an unrelated fact on slug collision. (1) Removed --supersedes from the existing-inherit load (engine.ts ~1521) so a new fact never adopts a collision victim's type/frontmatter. (2) Added a slug-existence guard in the supersede block: since supersede skips all dedup tiers, if the derived/--as slug already names a DIFFERENT fact (self-supersede already guarded above), reject with ClientError 'slug X already names a different fact — pass --as to disambiguate' (400). Victim byte-preserved; target not retired on the rejected run. +1 test, 1018 green.

title: Tag with embedded double quote breaks flow-seq round-trip — following tags silently merge
description: VERIFIED (3-lens adversarial, reproduced against exported serializeMemory/parseMemory). src/engine.ts:260: needsTagQuoting only fires on a LEADING quote (needsYamlQuoting anchors /^[\s"...]/), so a tag with mid/trailing unpaired double-quote serializes bare, while splitFlowSeq toggles on every quote char. tags [a-quote-x, b] round-trips to the single merged tag — tag b disappears from listFacts --tag, tagHistogram, session snapshot; the --replace inherit path re-serializes the merged form, making it permanent. Fix: quote any tag containing a double quote (or escape in splitFlowSeq).

title: Test hygiene batch: env leak into spawned CLI, unpinned REST contract, untested session pull, .test.ts never typechecked
description: UNVERIFIED as batch (finder code-read; typecheck drift finder-verified by compiling). (1) test/cli.test.ts:441 runCli/runCliEnv spread ...process.env without clearing QMEMD_*/QMD_* — a dev host exporting QMEMD_RECALL_MODE=hybrid makes the QMD_FORCE_CPU-forces-lex test go hybrid and probe the real daemon on 127.0.0.1:8182. Clear mode vars in the spawn env. (2) CLAUDE.md bold contract REST no project => no gate => whole corpus has no pinning test — http.test.ts:269 only tests project:alpha + crossProject:true; add a POST /recall without project asserting foreign facts return. (3) CLI recall --session pull site (src/cli/qmemd.ts:292) and REST session path (src/mcp/server.ts:640) have zero pull coverage — only the MCP tool branch is tested (mcp.test.ts:626). (4) Regular .test.ts (11k lines) excluded from every tsc program and already fail strict (TS7016 test/plugin.test.ts:8-9 implicit-any .mjs imports); decide: include tests in typecheck program or accept and document. (5) test/daemon-cli.test.ts:53 detach+live-kill path is skipIf(CI) — never runs in GitHub Actions; hardcoded port locally.

title: Tier-2 dedup aborts an unrelated remember on a malformed index row
description:
engine.ts:1543: Tier-2 dedup resolves an index-row filepath via slugFromFilepath (whose fallback can return a multi-segment path containing /) and calls getFact OUTSIDE any try, and casts dupType as MemoryType unvalidated (1534). CONFIRMED: a stale/malformed row (e.g. qmd://memory/user/.md from an out-of-band file adopted via qmemd reindex) that becomes the top-1 lex hit for a new remember makes slugFromFilepath return memory/user/.md -> getFact -> assertSafeSlug throws unsafe slug -> the whole unrelated remember aborts until the index is rebuilt. recallQuery toHit already validates type membership and skips such rows with a stderr nudge (qmemd-4ri); Tier-2 does not.

Fix direction: reuse parseVirtualMemoryPath + the toHit validate-and-skip pattern (and wrap the classification getFact in try/catch, as duplicatePreview already does) so a bad row is skipped, not fatal.

title: Tier-2.5 near-dup scan is corpus-wide — same-phrasing fact for another project blocked
description: UNVERIFIED (finder claims repro: Dice 0.857 >= 0.82, no identifier/polarity signal, disposition duplicate). src/engine.ts:1604: scanFacts pass has no project scoping, so remembering a beta-project fact phrased like an existing alpha-project fact is withheld. Recall gates by project (qmemd-due) but dedup does not — asymmetric. Fix: scope Tier-2.5 (and Tier-2 FTS?) to {fact project, global}, or downgrade cross-project near-dups to a surfaced conflict instead of a silent block.

title: reviewByFromTtl has no upper bound — huge ttl throws RangeError, env override bricks stale/doctor
description:
engine.ts:132 reviewByFromTtl does no range check, so a shape-valid huge ttl overflows the Date range and .toISOString() throws an uncaught RangeError (Invalid time value) instead of the intended path-free invalid ttl client error. CONFIRMED (overflow reproduced): qmemd remember x --ttl 999999999d -> CLI raw stack + exit 1, REST 500, MCP internal error (message matches neither error allowlist). Worse, a parseable-huge env override QMEMD_TTL_PROJECT=3000000y flows through ttlDefaultDays (whose contract says fail-safe, never throws) into staleFacts, so qmemd stale AND qmemd doctor throw RangeError on the first never-reviewed project fact of an otherwise-healthy corpus.

Fix direction: bound parseTtlDays / reviewByFromTtl (reject or clamp days that would overflow the epoch) and return the path-free invalid ttl error; make ttlDefaultDays truly never-throw.
close_reason: Bounded parseTtlDays (engine.ts): a day count > 36_500_000 (~100,000y, well below the ±8.64e15ms Date epoch limit) now returns null instead of a huge finite number. Single choke point fixes BOTH surfaces: reviewByFromTtl(null) raises its intended 'invalid ttl' ClientError (now 400 via the f6j ClientError work) instead of an uncaught RangeError from new Date(overflow).toISOString(); ttlDefaultDays(null) falls back to the hardcoded default so a parseable-huge env override (QMEMD_TTL_PROJECT=3000000y) can no longer overflow staleFacts/doctor. +3 tests, 1021 green.

title: mcp uninstall-service --print ignores --print — deletes unit + daemon.env anyway
description: UNVERIFIED (finder code-read). src/cli/qmemd.ts:615: install-service --print is a documented zero-side-effect preview (line 598), but uninstall-service unconditionally runs removeArtifacts — systemd unit/launchd plist AND ~/.config/qmemd/daemon.env unlinked with no backup, including hand edits (PATH, QMEMD_EMBED_MODEL). Fix: honor --print symmetrically; consider backing up daemon.env on removal. Related family: qp-mcp-cli-daemon-hazards-q70.

title: Tier-2.5 dedup loop micro-efficiency: tokenizes newCompare per candidate (4x/candidate with lowSimilarityConflict), matched fact re-read up to 3x on return path
description: Deferred from qp-nq2 (structural dedup landed there; this is the perf remainder). engine.ts remember() Tier-2.5: nearDuplicate(newCompare, cand) re-tokenizes newCompare every candidate and lowSimilarityConflict tokenizes both again — ~4 tokenizations/candidate; a set-based internal core computed once would drop it to 1x/candidate. On a match, duplicatePreview + buildAuthorityComparison + (Tier-2) getFact re-read the same matched fact up to 3x. Corpus is tiny (scanFacts comment: microseconds), so this is polish, not a bug — do only with benchmarks or when touching the dedup seam anyway. NON-PORT guardrail applies.

title: beacon-split follow-ups: test hardening + comment restores (final-review minors)
description: From feat/beacon-repo-global-split final whole-branch review (all ship-as-is, bundled): (1) test/adversarial/security.test.ts:109 RecallHit literal missing project — pre-existing one-liner; (2) beacon exact-<=12-tags no-suffix boundary test (guard hidden>0, cheap regression pin); (3) CLI tags (untagged) fallback test + zero-repo/global>0 omission mirror test; (4) src/mcp/server.ts:417 comment rot: 'safe to expose wholesale' false since toListEntryDTO drops project — reword; (5) restore i34 5-line rationale comment in beacon.test.ts (dropped by plan-mandated block replacement); (6) consider widening tsconfig.typecheck.json to test/**/*.ts so type-invisible .test.ts debt stops hiding (needs hooks/*.mjs import handling).
